 {"id":"bd-zgme","title":"Implement dependency tree display","description":"## Task: Dependency Tree Display

### Parent Feature
Phase 3: Advanced Output Components (bd-1lkf)

### Overview
Create a visual tree display of the dependency injection graph, showing how dependencies are resolved and detecting circular dependencies.

### Background & Reasoning
Dependency injection can become complex in larger applications. Visualizing the dependency graph helps developers:
- Understand what depends on what
- Identify circular dependency issues before runtime
- Optimize resolution order
- Debug 'missing dependency' errors by seeing the full chain

### Technical Implementation

#### Data Structures
```rust
// crates/fastapi-output/src/components/dependency_tree.rs

pub struct DependencyNode {
    pub name: String,
    pub type_name: String,
    pub scope: DependencyScope,
    pub children: Vec<DependencyNode>,
    pub is_circular_ref: bool,
}

pub enum DependencyScope {
    Singleton,   // Application lifetime
    Request,     // Per-request
    Transient,   // New instance each time
}

pub struct DependencyTreeDisplay {
    roots: Vec<DependencyNode>,
    show_types: bool,
    show_scopes: bool,
    highlight_cycles: bool,
}
```

#### Visualization

**Rich Mode (Tree with Colors)**:
```
┌────────────── Dependency Graph ──────────────┐
│                                               │
│ DatabasePool (singleton)                      │
│ └── type: sqlx::Pool<Postgres>               │
│                                               │
│ UserService (request)                         │
│ ├── DatabasePool                              │
│ └── CacheService                              │
│     └── RedisPool (singleton)                 │
│                                               │
│ AuthService (request)                         │
│ ├── UserService                               │
│ └── TokenValidator (singleton)                │
│                                               │
│ ⚠ CYCLE DETECTED:                            │
│   ServiceA → ServiceB → ServiceA              │
└───────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

**Plain Mode**:
```
Dependency Graph:
  DatabasePool (singleton)
  UserService (request) <- DatabasePool, CacheService
  CacheService (request) <- RedisPool
  AuthService (request) <- UserService, TokenValidator
  WARNING: Circular dependency: ServiceA -> ServiceB -> ServiceA
```

#### Implementation
```rust
impl DependencyTreeDisplay {
    pub fn display(&self) {
        let output = RichOutput::global();
        
        if !output.should_render() {
            self.display_plain(&output);
            return;
        }
        
        let theme = output.theme();
        let mut tree_nodes = Vec::new();
        
        for root in &self.roots {
            tree_nodes.push(self.build_tree_node(root, theme, 0));
        }
        
        // Check for cycles
        let cycles = self.detect_cycles();
        
        let mut content_parts: Vec<String> = Vec::new();
        
        for node in tree_nodes {
            let tree = Tree::new(node);
            content_parts.push(format!("{}", tree.render_to_string()));
        }
        
        if !cycles.is_empty() {
            content_parts.push(String::new());
            content_parts.push(theme.style_text("⚠ CYCLES DETECTED:", "red bold"));
            for cycle in cycles {
                content_parts.push(format!("  {}", cycle.join(" → ")));
            }
        }
        
        let panel = Panel::from_text(&content_parts.join("\n"))
            .title("Dependency Graph")
            .rounded();
        
        output.renderable(&panel);
    }
    
    fn build_tree_node(&self, dep: &DependencyNode, theme: &FastApiTheme, depth: usize) -> TreeNode {
        let scope_indicator = match dep.scope {
            DependencyScope::Singleton => theme.style_text("(singleton)", "blue"),
            DependencyScope::Request => theme.style_text("(request)", "green"),
            DependencyScope::Transient => theme.style_text("(transient)", "yellow"),
        };
        
        let label = if dep.is_circular_ref {
            theme.style_text(&format!("{} {} ↻", dep.name, scope_indicator), "red")
        } else {
            format!("{} {}", dep.name, scope_indicator)
        };
        
        let mut node = TreeNode::new(label);
        
        if self.show_types {
            node.add_child(TreeNode::new(
                theme.style_text(&format!("type: {}", dep.type_name), "dim")
            ));
        }
        
        for child in &dep.children {
            node.add_child(self.build_tree_node(child, theme, depth + 1));
        }
        
        node
    }
    
    fn detect_cycles(&self) -> Vec<Vec<String>> {
        // DFS-based cycle detection
        let mut cycles = Vec::new();
        let mut visited = HashSet::new();
        let mut path = Vec::new();
        
        for root in &self.roots {
            self.find_cycles(root, &mut visited, &mut path, &mut cycles);
        }
        
        cycles
    }
}
```

### Integration with DI System
```rust
// In fastapi-core/src/depends.rs

impl DependencyContainer {
    pub fn display_graph(&self) {
        let roots = self.build_dependency_nodes();
        
        DependencyTreeDisplay {
            roots,
            show_types: true,
            show_scopes: true,
            highlight_cycles: true,
        }.display();
    }
}
```

### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] DependencyNode and DependencyTreeDisplay structs
- [ ] Recursive tree building with depth tracking
- [ ] Scope coloring (singleton=blue, request=green, transient=yellow)
- [ ] Circular dependency detection with DFS
- [ ] Cycle highlighting in red with ↻ indicator
- [ ] Type information display (optional)
- [ ] Plain text fallback
- [ ] Integration with DependencyContainer
- [ ] Unit tests for cycle detection

### Dependencies
- bd-36uw (RichOutput facade)
- bd-14v4 (FastApiTheme)
- Access to fastapi-core DependencyContainer

### Considerations
- Deep dependency trees should be truncated with '...' indicator
- Consider caching the graph visualization for large apps
- Cycle detection must handle self-referential deps","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T21:12:48.877004850Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-22T02:28:31.691597921Z","closed_at":"2026-01-22T02:28:31.691509544Z","close_reason":"Implemented - dependency_tree.rs complete with DependencyNode, DependencyTreeDisplay, cycle detection, and tests","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-zgme","depends_on_id":"bd-14v4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T00:10:31Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-zgme","depends_on_id":"bd-1lkf","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T00:10:31Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-zgme","depends_on_id":"bd-36uw","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T00:10:31Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"fastapi_rust-0ar","title":"Connection acceptance with limits","description":"Implement connection limiting in server:
- Max concurrent connections limit
- Per-IP connection limits (optional)
- Connection queue with backpressure
- Graceful rejection with 503 when at limit

DEPENDS ON: TCP server with asupersync

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- Server respects max_connections setting
- Excess connections rejected gracefully
- No resource exhaustion under load
- Metrics: current connection count

TEST REQUIREMENTS:
- Unit: Limit enforcement
- Integration: Behavior at limit
- Load: Many simultaneous connections
- Recovery: After connections close","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T14:39:42.844827923Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-21T11:17:21.648034840Z","closed_at":"2026-01-21T11:17:21.647979135Z","close_reason":"Implemented connection acceptance with limits:
- Added connection_counter AtomicU64 to TcpServer for tracking active connections
- Implemented try_acquire_connection() with compare-exchange for thread-safe limit checking
- Implemented release_connection() for decrementing counter
- Added current_connections() public method for metrics
- Connection limit enforced in accept_loop before handling
- Returns 503 Service Unavailable with Connection: close when limit reached
- Added 8 unit tests for connection limiting behavior","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["limits","phase-1","server"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"fastapi_rust-0ar","depends_on_id":"fastapi_rust-9ik","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T00:10:31Z","created_by":"import"}]}
-{"id":"fastapi_rust-0mi","title":"Content negotiation via Accept headers","description":"Support server-driven content negotiation via Accept, Accept-Encoding, Accept-Language headers. Allow handlers to return different representations based on client preferences.

**Implementation:**
- Parse Accept header with quality values (q=)
- Parse Accept-Encoding for compression negotiation
- Parse Accept-Language for localization
- Content-Type selection based on Accept
- Vary header generation
- 406 Not Acceptable when no match

**Components:**
- AcceptHeader extractor with parsed preferences
- negotiate_content_type() helper
- Vary header auto-generation
- Integration with response serialization

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- Accept: application/json,text/html;q=0.9 prefers JSON
- Accept-Encoding respected for compression
- Vary header includes Accept when used
- 406 when requested type not available
- Wildcard matching works

**Tests:**
- Handler returns JSON for Accept: application/json
- Handler returns HTML for Accept: text/html
- Quality values respected in ordering
- 406 for unsupported Accept type
- Vary header included in response","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T16:12:53.745765535Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-29T00:49:22.573660461Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["http","negotiation","phase-2"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"fastapi_rust-0mi","depends_on_id":"fastapi_rust-d6d","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T00:10:31Z","created_by":"import"}]}

**Implementation:**
- Parse Accept header with quality values (q=)
- Parse Accept-Encoding for compression negotiation
- Parse Accept-Language for localization
- Content-Type selection based on Accept
- Vary header generation

**Components:**
- AcceptHeader extractor with parsed preferences

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- Accept: application/json,text/html;q=0.9 prefers JSON
- Accept-Encoding respected for compression
- Vary header includes Accept when used
- 406 when requested type not available
- Wildcard matching works

**Tests:**
- Handler returns JSON for Accept: application/json
- Handler returns HTML for Accept: text/html
- Quality values respected in ordering
- 406 for unsupported Accept type
- Vary header included in response","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T16:12:53.745765535Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-29T00:56:54.154932973Z","closed_at":"2026-01-29T00:56:54.154867280Z","close_reason":"Implemented content negotiation via Accept headers:

## Added Types
- MediaType: Parsed media type with type/subtype/parameters
- AcceptItem: Single Accept header entry with quality value  
- AcceptHeader: Full Accept header extractor with negotiation methods
- AcceptEncodingItem/AcceptEncodingHeader: Accept-Encoding support
- AcceptLanguageItem/AcceptLanguageHeader: Accept-Language support
- NotAcceptableError: 406 Not Acceptable error response
- VaryBuilder: Helper for building Vary response headers

## Features
- Parse Accept header with quality values (q=)
- Parse Accept-Encoding for compression negotiation
- Parse Accept-Language for localization
- Content-Type selection based on Accept (negotiate method)
- Quality value comparisons and ordering
- Wildcard matching (*/*, text/*, etc.)
- Vary header generation helper
- 406 Not Acceptable response on negotiation failure

## Tests
- 21 unit tests covering all functionality
- Parse tests for Accept, Accept-Encoding, Accept-Language
- Negotiation tests for content type selection
- Quality value and wildcard matching tests
- VaryBuilder and NotAcceptableError tests

Also added StatusCode::NOT_ACCEPTABLE (406) to response.rs.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["http","negotiation","phase-2"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"fastapi_rust-0mi","depends_on_id":"fastapi_rust-d6d","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T00:10:31Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"fastapi_rust-0qq","title":"Link headers for HATEOAS and pagination","description":"Support Link headers for HATEOAS-style API navigation and pagination links. Helps clients discover related resources.

Implementation:
- Link header builder
- rel types: self, next, prev, first, last, related
- Multiple links in one header
- URL templating support
- Integration with pagination helpers

Link Types:
- next/prev for pagination
- self for canonical URL
- related for associated resources
- alternate for different representations

Acceptance Criteria:
- Multiple links in single header
- Proper RFC 8288 format
- Integration with pagination
- URL encoding handled correctly
- Easy builder API

Tests:
- Pagination adds next/prev links
- Link header properly formatted
- Multiple rel values work
- Special characters in URLs escaped","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T16:15:13.214282902Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T16:15:13.214282902Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["hateoas","headers","pagination","response"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"fastapi_rust-0qq","depends_on_id":"fastapi_rust-rbz","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T00:10:31Z","created_by":"import"}]}

- Support multiple: Path<(String, i64)>

- Proper error messages on type conversion failure

DEPENDS ON: Router path parameters

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- Path<T> works with all supported types
- Clear error for missing/invalid params
- Works with serde derive
- Zero-copy where possible

- Unit: Each type extraction
- Unit: Error messages
- Unit: Multiple params
- Integration: With router","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T14:42:22.325927347Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-18T06:39:51.632976576Z","closed_at":"2026-01-18T06:39:51.632976576Z","close_reason":"Implemented Path<T> extractor with PathParams storage, PathDeserializer for serde integration, support for single values/tuples/structs, comprehensive error handling, and 17 unit tests.
